feat: backfill string-anchored facts with transitory entities [bu-cbs.4] (#530)

* feat: add backfill script for string-anchored facts with transitory entities [bu-cbs.4]

- scripts/backfill_transitory_entities.py: diagnostic + apply script that
  finds active facts with entity_id IS NULL (excluding generic labels like
  Owner/user/me), infers entity_type from scope and subject tokens, creates
  transitory entities in shared.entities with metadata={unidentified:true,
  source:backfill, source_butler, source_scope}, and links all matching facts
  - Dry-run mode (default) prints per-schema diagnostic without writing
  - --apply flag commits the backfill in one transaction per schema
  - Handles UniqueViolationError by resolving the existing entity (idempotent)
  - --schema flag targets a single butler schema; otherwise auto-discovers
    all schemas with a facts table via information_schema

- tests/scripts/test_backfill_transitory_entities.py: 35 unit tests covering
  infer_entity_type heuristics, run_diagnostic, resolve_or_create_entity,
  backfill_schema (dry-run and apply), discover_memory_schemas, and generic
  label exclusion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: validate schema name to prevent SQL injection in backfill script

Add _validate_schema_name() that rejects any identifier not matching
[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*, called before both f-string SQL interpolations
(SET search_path and UPDATE {schema}.facts). Also fix misleading comment
in infer_entity_type() for the relationship scope branch.

Addresses PR review threads:
- PRRT_kwDORLxgpc5zMNsW (SET search_path SQL injection)
- PRRT_kwDORLxgpc5zMNsZ (UPDATE f-string SQL injection)
- PRRT_kwDORLxgpc5zMNsd (misleading comment on person-scope logic)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
[FIX] PlaylistsClient — fix API contract mismatches and React anti-patterns

- Fix fetch URL: /items sub-resource → GET /{id} (correct FastAPI endpoint)
- Fix reorder: POST → PATCH, ordered_clip_ids → item_ids (PI{5D} keys)
- Fix useSortable id: clip_id → item_id (DnD context uses item_id)
- Fix SortableContext items: clip_id → item_id
- Fix position display: position field → sort_order + 1
- Fix async side-effect inside setState → clean synchronous optimistic update
- Fix optional ClipRow fields (start_time, end_time, r2_url) with safe defaults
- Install @dnd-kit/core, @dnd-kit/sortable, @dnd-kit/utilities packages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: clip generation in headless mode (close #254) (#256)

* fix: use absolute paths in ffmpeg concat filelist

When generating clips with multiple ranges, ensure video paths in the
filelist are always absolute. This prevents FFmpeg from misinterpreting
relative paths, which caused double-nested path issues in Docker/headless
mode where config paths might be relative (e.g., './output').

Using canonicalize() ensures paths are absolute regardless of how they
were originally specified in the config.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use async canonicalize and log failures in filelist

commit.message:
Speed up Vite dev server: gzip compression + game metadata caching (#982)

## Summary

- **Gzip compression for dev server**: The Vite dev server now
compresses SSR responses with gzip, reducing transfer sizes by 76-97%
(e.g. `/games` goes from 1.2 MB to 38 KB)
- **Cache game metadata in globalThis**: The three game-listing pages
(`/games`, `/season/*/games`, `/season/*/results`) each read and parsed
all ~375 game JSON files (~1 GB) on every SSR request, taking 4-5
seconds. Extracted the shared scanning logic into `load-games.ts` which
caches on `globalThis` — first page load takes ~4s, subsequent loads are
instant for the lifetime of the Vite dev server process
- **Dev-mode only**: No changes to the Cloudflare Pages build path or
Python pipeline. The `globalThis` cache is a Node process-level
optimization that only helps the Vite dev server

## Test plan

- [x] `make check` passes (lint, typecheck, tests)
- [x] Verified `/games`, `/season/3/games`, `/season/3/results` all
render correctly
- [x] Verified first load ~4s, subsequent loads <100ms
- [x] Verified gzip Content-Encoding in response headers

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: Bitfinex ROI estimation + PnL-only arena score

Bitfinex API only returns absolute PnL (no ROI%). Previously all 551
traders got arena_score=null, capping effective score at 0.

Two-pronged fix:
1. Fetch 'plu' (inception unrealized profit) as account equity proxy,
   then estimate ROI = period_pnl / equity * 100 for overlapping traders
2. For remaining traders, compute PnL-only arena score (max 40/100)
   instead of null — using calculateArenaScore(0, pnl, ...) so PnL
   component still contributes

commit.message:
fix: HTX futures PnL extraction - use copyProfit fallback for zero profit90 traders

Root cause: ~32% of HTX traders have profit90="0.000000" (inactive in 90D personal
trading) but have non-zero copyProfit. The old code `Number(item.profit90 || ...) || null`
failed because: (1) "0.000000" is a truthy string so it short-circuits the || chain,
(2) Number("0.000000") = 0 which then becomes null via `0 || null`.

Fix: Parse both profit90 and copyProfit as numbers, prefer non-zero profit90, fall back
to copyProfit when profit90 is zero. Also fixes followers field (copyUserNum=0 → null).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: set bybit/bybit_spot PnL to null — mv[2] is followerProfit not trader PnL

The Bybit leaderboard API metricValues[2] is "followerProfit" (copier PnL),
not the trader's own PnL. Using this wrong value corrupted arena scores and
rankings. Set pnl=null to avoid wrong data; real trader PnL comes from the
per-trader detail endpoint during enrichment.

commit.message:
Apply AGENTS.md style guidelines across the codebase

Writing:
- Fix 8 doc titles to use Title Case per writing guidelines
- Update mkdocs.yml nav titles to match

Coding (Google C++ Style Guide):
- Add IncludeCategories to .clang-format to enforce correct include
  ordering (C++ standard → third-party → project)
- Mark multi-parameter constructors explicit (Abseil TotW #142)
- Add [[nodiscard]] to checked_cast, checked_shl, checked_shr, and
  Int::checked — ignoring these return values would be a bug
- Replace C-style macros (INT64_MIN, etc.) with std::numeric_limits
- Use std::tuple consistently instead of std::pair for multi-value returns

DRY:
- Deduplicate BitVec comparison operators (<=, >, >=) by delegating to <
- Remove duplicated static_assert in BitVec::Literal

commit.message:
fix: increase Hyperliquid enrichment to cover all 500 traders

ENRICH_LIMIT was 300 while TARGET was 500, leaving 200 traders per
period with null win_rate and max_drawdown (~40% missing coverage).

- ENRICH_LIMIT: 300 → 500 (match TARGET for 100% coverage)
- CONCURRENCY: 8 → 12 (compensate for larger batch)
- DELAY_MS: 100 → 50 (reduce idle time between batches)

Estimated timing: ~42 batches × ~2s avg = ~84s/period, ~252s for 3
periods, well within 300s serverless timeout.

- Line 2: last user prompt (bright status-colored)
- Line 3: current agent activity (uniform grey, non-distracting)
- Line 4: CPU/elapsed/context OR "NEEDS HUMAN INTERVENTION"

Uses incremental JSONL reading for efficient token counting on large files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Speed up WS connection for leader toggle — process registrations first, scale stagger

Three changes to reduce WS connection time after toggling auto-copy:

1. Process pending registrations at START of main loop iteration (not just
   after 10s asyncio.wait). If leader's token already has a live connection
   (shared with position monitor), on_connected() fires immediately.

2. Scale stagger delay by number of disconnected connections:
   - 1-2 connections: 0-5s (single toggle, low 429 risk)
   - 3-5 connections: 0-15s (moderate boot)
   - 6+ connections: 0-30s (full deploy — Bug #38 protection preserved)
   Semaphore(2) + 3s spacing remains PRIMARY 429 protection (Bug #40).

3. Reduce main loop sleep 2s→1s for faster registration pickup.

Expected: leader toggle connects in 3-8s (was 15-45s).

commit.message:
fix: respect Content-Type charset in Gmail body extraction [bu-791]

Adds `_charset_from_headers()` and `_decode_part_bytes()` helpers to
`GmailConnectorRuntime`. Both the text/plain and text/html extraction
paths now decode base64url bytes with the charset declared in the MIME
part's Content-Type header (via `codecs.lookup()` for validation),
falling back to UTF-8 with replacement characters for absent or
unrecognised charsets.

Adds two new tests:
- `test_extract_body_charset_iso8859` — ISO-8859-1 encoded body decoded correctly
- `test_extract_body_charset_fallback` — unknown charset falls back to UTF-8 gracefully

commit.message:
fix: kill process trees before destroying tmux windows to prevent dev server leaks

Agents (spotters/leads) start dev servers for validation but don't always
clean them up, leaving orphaned processes consuming memory and ports.

Two-layer fix:
- Primary: spotter.md now instructs agents to stop all dev servers before
  writing SPOT.json
- Safety net: Go-side killPaneProcessTree recursively finds and SIGTERMs
  all descendant processes before KillWindow, with a 300ms grace period

Hydrophone job resume after restart, cache invalidation, periodic stale recovery

Hydrophone detection jobs now resume from segments_processed checkpoint
instead of reprocessing from scratch. Prior detections are read from TSV,
already-processed segments are skipped, and timeline changes are guarded
against. Corrupted cached .ts segments are invalidated and re-fetched on
decode failure. Worker loop runs stale job recovery every 60s instead of
only at startup, fixing jobs stuck in running after quick restarts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
